tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Exonerated After Three Years, Greg Kelly Runs 100 Miles to Reclaim His Life
stories.summary: Greg Kelly, wrongfully imprisoned for three years, completed a grueling 100-mile run through Huntsville to reclaim his freedom and fight for others unjustly convicted.

stories.headline: A Father's Quick Thinking Saves a Stranger's Life at the Park
stories.summary: Jeff Gulla, who donated blood that morning, instinctively performed CPR on a collapsed tennis player, helping save his life with neighbors and first responders.

stories.headline: After 60 Years Gone, Grey Hornbills Return Home to India's Forests
stories.summary: Indian Grey Hornbills have returned to Gujarat's Gir forests after 60 years, breeding successfully for four consecutive years and restoring this vital seed-dispersing species.

stories.headline: Once Nearly Lost, Rare Bonneted Bats Now Thrive in Florida
stories.summary: Frank Ridgley and his team at Zoo Miami brought Florida's endangered bonneted bats back from the brink, documenting over 200—the world's largest population—in specially designed homes.

stories.headline: From 99% Decimated to 9,000 Strong: Sea Turtles Make an Incredible Comeback
stories.summary: Kemp's ridley sea turtles, nearly wiped out by 1985, now number thousands of nesting females thanks to 30 years of international protection and community beach care.

stories.headline: Community Rallies to Return Stolen Farm Stand to Young Farmer
stories.summary: Neighbors who barely knew a young farmer came together online to recover her stolen pink farm stand, thrilled to help bring it back to their community.

stories.headline: Hundreds Rush to Help After Apartment Fire Leaves Families With Nothing
stories.summary: New Westminster residents, led by New West Neighbourhood House, flooded Lord Kelvin Community School's gymnasium within 48 hours with donations for dozens of families who lost everything in a July 10 apartment fire.

stories.headline: Rural Neighbors Spring Into Action During Wildfire Crisis
stories.summary: Southern Oregon ranchers and community members instantly mobilized to shelter evacuees, rescue livestock, and feed families fleeing the East Evans Creek Fire without being asked.

stories.headline: A Surgeon, a Robot, and a Gallbladder: The Future of Surgery Arrives
stories.summary: Surgie, a teleoperated humanoid robot controlled by surgeon Michael Yip, successfully removed a gallbladder in an operating room, proving remote surgery is now within reach.

stories.headline: Last-Second Magic: Jordan Walker Wins Home Run Derby With Six Final Homers
stories.summary: St. Louis Cardinals' Jordan Walker became the first Cardinal to win the Home Run Derby, hitting six consecutive final homers to stun the crowd and earn a $1 million prize.

stories.headline: West African Leopards Bounce Back From the Brink
stories.summary: Once critically endangered, West African leopards are thriving again in Benin's Pendjari National Park, with populations rising steadily thanks to dedicated conservation partnership.

stories.headline: Nature's Tiny Helpers: Microbes Clean Up Uranium Pollution
stories.summary: Native bacteria transformed dissolved uranium into solid form, naturally removing 96% of contamination from flooded mine water in a remarkable cleanup breakthrough.
